title: Spinback Effect
description: Use a reverse jog wheel spin to create a dramatic spinback — a classic DJ effect that signals the end of a section.

steps.instruction: Watch: Deck A plays normally. At the transition point, the jog wheel spins backward hard — a fast reverse.
Watch: as the spinback plays, the crossfader cuts to Deck B. The new track launches over the reverse sound.
Watch: Deck A's volume is brought down immediately after the spinback. Deck B plays solo.

phases.instruction: Imagine grabbing a spinning record and whipping it backwards — that high-pitched reverse squeal is the spinback. It is theatrical and definitive. It says: 'THIS part is done, and THAT part begins.' It is best used at major structural breaks, not random moments.

steps.instruction: Set up both decks: Deck A playing at 80% volume, Deck B beatmatched and ready, volume at zero.
Identify a strong transition moment on Deck A — the end of a phrase, the last beat of a breakdown.
At the transition point — grab the jog wheel and spin it backward hard and fast. Feel the reverse sound.
While the spinback plays — bring Deck B's volume up to 80% immediately.
Snap the crossfader to Deck B. Deck A's reverse fades into silence as Deck B takes over.
Bring Deck A's volume all the way down. The spinback is done. Deck B is the mix.
